title: Capstone: Complete Customer Activity Summary
subtitle: Preserve customers, enrich dimensions, and aggregate transactions safely
learning_objective: Build a complete one-row-per-customer summary while preserving customers with no region or orders.
why: A common analyst deliverable is a complete customer dimension enriched with activity metrics and explicit zeroes.
prompt: Return `customer_id`, `customer_name`, `region_name`, `completed_order_count`, `completed_revenue`, and `latest_order_date` for every customer. Display `Unassigned` when no region matches. Count and sum only completed orders, but calculate latest_order_date from any order. Order by `customer_id`.
expected_result: 6 rows, one per customer. Customers 5 and 6 have 0 completed orders, 0 completed revenue, and NULL latest_order_date.
build_steps: Start from customers because every customer must remain.
LEFT JOIN regions for the readable region name.
Use CASE inside COUNT and SUM to include completed orders only.
Group by customer and region fields.
explanation: Two left joins preserve the customer set. Conditional aggregation computes selected metrics while MAX can still inspect every order status.
common_mistakes: Filtering completed orders in WHERE, which removes customers with no completed orders.
Using INNER JOIN to regions and losing the unassigned customer.
Counting joined rows without restricting to completed orders.
Forgetting that SUM may need an ELSE 0 branch.
hints: Use `COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN o.status = 'Completed' THEN o.order_id END)`.
Use `SUM(CASE WHEN ... THEN o.total_amount ELSE 0 END)`.
`MAX(o.order_date)` should not be limited to completed status.
takeaways: Conditional aggregation is safer than filtering away zero-activity entities.
State the final grain: one row per customer.
reflection_questions: Why is the status condition inside CASE instead of WHERE?
Which join preserves Evergreen Health despite a missing region?
stretch_goal: Add a `has_pending_order` flag using `MAX(CASE WHEN status = 'Pending' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)`.
starter_sql:
SELECT
    c.customer_id,
    c.customer_name,
    COALESCE(r.region_name, 'Unassigned') AS region_name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE
        WHEN -- completed condition
        THEN o.order_id
    END) AS completed_order_count,
    SUM(CASE
        WHEN -- completed condition
        THEN o.total_amount
        ELSE 0
    END) AS completed_revenue,
    MAX(o.order_date) AS latest_order_date
FROM customers AS c
LEFT JOIN regions AS r
    ON -- region relationship
LEFT JOIN orders AS o
    ON -- customer relationship
GROUP BY -- customer and region columns
ORDER BY c.customer_id;
solution_explanation: Customers remain the preserved grain while left joins add optional dimensions and transactions; conditional aggregation produces completed metrics without dropping zero-activity customers.
solution_walkthrough: Customers establish the six-row preserved population.
The region join enriches five customers and leaves one unmatched.
The order join creates transaction-level rows while retaining inactive customers.
CASE expressions count and sum only completed orders.
GROUP BY collapses activity back to one row per customer.
